title: Final bounded verdict for research instrumentation proof case
summary: This proof case now supports one bounded conclusion: the recovered research/null-task_type alert is better explained and handled as an instrumentation-first case than as a defensible skill regression. The case is no longer an empty scaffold, but it still falls short of a strong closed-loop proof because repeated downstream evidence is missing.
one_line_takeaway: Bounded proof achieved: live evidence supports instrumentation-first suppression of a thin-evidence regression candidate, not a strong claim of durable closed-loop improvement.

core_claim.statement: For this proof case, the selected segmentation/outcome-consistency gate is active on the live system and suppresses the reconstructed thin-evidence research/null-task_type candidate, supporting an instrumentation-first interpretation over a direct skill-regression reading.

what_is_supported: The trigger case is real enough to analyze but epistemically weak as a raw skill-regression claim.
A concrete conservative intervention was selected and tied to live code plus grounded git references.
A bounded live measurement shows the ungated counterfactual candidate still exists while the gated path suppresses it.
The current best reading is measurement-artifact leaning / instrumentation-first.
what_is_not_supported: A strong claim that the research skill truly regressed.
A broad claim of durable downstream review-quality improvement.
A full end-to-end closed-loop proof with repeated post-change evidence.
A justification for routing-default mutation or skill demotion from this case alone.

publication_posture.public_safe_summary: One bounded proof case now exists for the claim that conservative signal gating can prevent thin-evidence escalation. It should be presented as a narrow operational result, not as proof of durable self-improvement.
publication_posture.oversell_guardrail: Do not describe this case as a strong closed-loop proof or as evidence of broad autonomous improvement.

remaining_gaps: Repeated observation window for downstream review-quality effects.
Broader post-change evidence beyond one bounded live check.
Historical provenance repair for legacy rows if stronger causal claims are needed.

next_options.option: stop here for public blueprint purposes
next_options.when_to_choose: if the goal is one honest bounded proof case rather than a stronger causal demonstration

next_options.option: run repeated candidate-emission checks over time
next_options.when_to_choose: if stronger confidence about downstream operational effect is needed

next_options.option: build a cleaner future worked example from a fresh signal onward
next_options.when_to_choose: if the goal is a more publication-friendly end-to-end closed-loop example
